diagnosis: The inner loop is performing well with steady improvements (10/11 iterations kept, best bpb decreasing consistently). It's correctly focusing on learning rate parameters that show momentum signals and using crossover effectively. However, it's exploring too many parameters simultaneously and not systematically isolating effects. The search is becoming slightly scattered with recent explorations of WARMDOWN_RATIO and batch parameters when learning rates are still showing clear improvement signals.
guidance: Focus exclusively on the four learning rate parameters: MATRIX_LR, SCALAR_LR, EMBEDDING_LR, UNEMBEDDING_LR. Use a systematic isolation approach: pick one parameter to vary by 5-15% while holding the other three at their values from the current best configuration (iter 9). After testing all four individually, explore coordinated pairs: MATRIX_LR+SCALAR_LR as one pair (maintaining their successful ratio from elites), and EMBEDDING_LR+UNEMBEDDING_LR as another pair (exploring inverse relationships). Do not touch any other parameters until this learning rate isolation sweep is complete. Use crossover only between configurations that follow this isolation pattern.
reasoning: Learning rate parameters have driven all improvements (7/7 attempts kept for MATRIX_LR and SCALAR_LR, 1/1 for EMBEDDING/UNEMBEDDING). Batch and schedule parameters have shown no clear benefit (0/1 kept for TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE, WARMUP_RATIO, FINAL_LR_FRAC). The search needs more systematic isolation to understand individual parameter effects before exploring interactions. Freezing non-learning-rate parameters prevents distraction from the most promising search direction. The focused strategy remains appropriate since we're still finding improvements, but needs tighter constraints.
